sources.citation: Thrun, Burgard & Fox. Probabilistic Robotics. MIT Press, 2005.
Sutton & Barto. RL: An Introduction 2nd ed. MIT Press, 2018.

learning_objectives: L1: Define DoF, kinematics, and the sense-plan-act cycle.
L1: Identify roles of LIDAR, camera, IMU, encoders, and actuators.
L1: State the difference between localisation, mapping, and SLAM.
L2: Explain FK and IK and describe where each is used.
L2: Describe how a particle filter estimates robot position under uncertainty.
L2: Explain the four AV stack levels: perception, localisation, planning, control.
L2: Compare deliberative, reactive, and hybrid architectures.
L3: Design a SLAM pipeline for a differential-drive robot in an unknown indoor environment.
L3: Evaluate ethical and safety trade-offs of deploying an autonomous system in public.

concepts.title: DoF and Sense-Plan-Act
concepts.explanation: DoF: independent parameters to fully describe configuration. Sense-Plan-Act cycle at 10–1000 Hz. Safety-critical systems run parallel loops.
concepts.worked_example: Doorknob=1 DoF, floor robot=3(x,y,θ), drone=6, UR5 arm=6, human arm≈7 (redundant for obstacle avoidance).

concepts.title: Sensors and Actuators
concepts.explanation: Proprioceptive: encoders (drift), IMU (drift). Exteroceptive: LIDAR (0.01m, 200m, fog issues), camera (lighting issues), radar (all-weather, low res), ultrasonic (short range). AVs fuse all.
concepts.worked_example: Encoder drifts 1m/10m. Camera fails in low light. GPS fails indoors. Sensor fusion is required.

concepts.title: FK and IK
concepts.explanation: FK: joint angles→end-effector pose. Always unique. IK: desired pose→joint angles. 0/1/multiple solutions. IK used in programming: specify where to move, not joint angles.
concepts.worked_example: UR5: FK confirms reachability; IK computes 6 joint angles. Multiple solutions (elbow-up/down) — nearest config selected.

concepts.title: RRT and Potential Fields
concepts.explanation: C-space: robot as point, obstacles as regions. RRT: sample random configs, extend tree — probabilistically complete. RRT*: asymptotically optimal. Potential fields: fast but local minima risk.
concepts.worked_example: 6-DoF arm: RRT finds path in ~50ms. Smoothed and time-parameterised before execution.

concepts.title: Particle Filter Localisation
concepts.explanation: N weighted particles. Predict (motion model+noise) → Update (weight by sensor likelihood) → Resample (proportional to weights). Converges when particles cluster at true pose.
concepts.worked_example: 500 particles. After 3 LIDAR scans: 95% within 0.1m of true position.

concepts.title: SLAM
concepts.explanation: Build map while localising. Pose graph: nodes=poses, edges=relative measurements. Visual SLAM (ORB-SLAM3): sparse map. LiDAR SLAM (Cartographer): dense map. Loop closure corrects drift.
concepts.worked_example: Cleaning robot: 10-min SLAM, <0.05m error. Loop closure corrects 0.3m accumulated drift.

concepts.title: AV Stack
concepts.explanation: (1) Perception: detect/classify from sensor fusion. (2) Localisation: HD map+SLAM+GPS, cm accuracy. (3) Planning: route (A*), behaviour, motion. (4) Control: PID or MPC. Safety: redundancy and fallback.
concepts.worked_example: AV at intersection: cyclist at 40m detected → yield → decelerate 50→0 km/h over 30m.

concepts.title: Robot Ethics
concepts.explanation: Responsibility gap, trolley problem in AVs, surveillance, labour displacement. Frameworks: IEEE Ethically Aligned Design, ISO 26262, EU AI Act (autonomous mobile robots = high-risk).
concepts.worked_example: Delivery robot on footpath: EU AI Act high-risk → conformity assessment and human oversight required.
